directions.name: AI Lab Research Notebook
avoidFor: Consumer shopping and simple local business sites
risks: Can feel academic if product action is not visible.
directions.firstViewport: Research statement, benchmark or diagram, model/product artifact, direct technical CTA.
layoutRules: Use figures, tables, code, and benchmark blocks.
Keep experimental visuals readable and grounded.
directions.typography: Research-paper clarity with modern product polish.

directions.name: Direct Response Product Detail
risks: A dramatic product story can push essential buying information too far down the page.
directions.firstViewport: Large product moment with price, variant selection, benefit proof, fulfillment promise, and persistent purchase action.
layoutRules: Keep selection, price, delivery, and purchase action adjacent.
Sequence proof from tangible benefit to specification and reassurance.
directions.typography: Confident product display type with crisp transactional labels and specifications.

legacyReferences.label: Apple
legacyReferences.role: focused product story and purchasing clarity

legacyReferences.label: Bugatti
legacyReferences.role: premium material presence and dramatic detail

directions.name: Marketplace Discovery Grid
risks: Inconsistent imagery and metadata can make a marketplace feel untrustworthy.
directions.firstViewport: Search-and-filter catalog with varied product cards, seller context, collection shortcuts, and visible sort state.
layoutRules: Make filtering reversible and preserve discovery context.
Keep product, price, seller, and delivery signals comparable.
directions.typography: Retail UI typography optimized for scanning names, prices, and seller metadata.

legacyReferences.label: Nike
legacyReferences.role: strong category rhythm and product imagery

legacyReferences.label: Shopify
legacyReferences.role: scalable catalog and trustworthy transaction patterns

directions.name: Commerce Premium Catalog
risks: Needs product assets; placeholders will break the premium feel.
directions.firstViewport: Product-first hero, price or collection signal, clear purchase or browse path.
layoutRules: Use real product imagery and stable product grids.
Do not hide purchase affordances behind decorative layouts.
directions.typography: Retail-readable hierarchy with crisp product metadata.

directions.name: Wholesale Order Workbench
risks: Consumer-style product cards slow down high-volume ordering workflows.
directions.firstViewport: Account-aware order table with SKU search, contract pricing, availability, quantity editing, and a persistent order summary.
layoutRules: Optimize for keyboard entry and repeated purchasing.
Keep account terms, stock, and order impact visible while editing.
directions.typography: Dense tabular typography with strong SKU, price, and quantity alignment.

legacyReferences.label: Airtable
legacyReferences.role: dense table operations and configurable views

directions.name: Consumer Brand Story
avoidFor: Dense admin tools and technical documentation
risks: Needs strong imagery; otherwise it feels like a generic landing page.
directions.firstViewport: Brand name, emotional promise, image-led product or lifestyle signal, simple CTA.
layoutRules: Use real or generated visual assets.
Avoid dashboard chrome unless the product is software-led.
directions.typography: Expressive heading paired with clear body and CTA text.

directions.name: Consumer Centered Hero Community
avoidFor: Interfaces whose primary emphasis conflicts with this direction.
Projects that require a different information-density policy.
risks: Balanced visual treatment still requires domain-specific content and evidence.
Expressive motion can distract from product evidence when overused.
Spacious composition can hide important comparison context when content grows.
directions.firstViewport: Use a centered opening statement followed by one concrete proof surface. Keep participation, shared activity, and belonging visually dominant.
layoutRules: Use a centered opening statement followed by one concrete proof surface.
Keep participation, shared activity, and belonging visually dominant.
Use balanced spacing with clear grouping and moderate information density.
Use restrained motion for continuity, feedback, and state changes only.
directions.typography: Humanist sans-serif typography with approachable rhythm and clear labels.

layoutRules: Prioritize availability, distance, price, and trust.
Keep the browse-to-book path short and reversible.

legacyReferences.role: local discovery and trustworthy provider context

legacyReferences.label: Uber

risks: A dense wall of artwork can make hierarchy and accessibility inconsistent.

layoutRules: Let cover art and collections define the information architecture.
Preserve readable controls over expressive media color.

legacyReferences.role: visual collection browsing and taste signals

legacyReferences.label: Spotify
legacyReferences.role: personalized media discovery and cultural energy

directions.name: Wellness Companion Soft
risks: Overly soft language may weaken credibility for evidence-based health products.
directions.firstViewport: Soft daily ritual story with a reassuring promise, human moment, simple progress cue, and low-pressure signup.
layoutRules: Use calm pacing and concrete daily benefits.
Keep sensitive progress data supportive rather than competitive.
directions.typography: Rounded, warm headings with highly readable supportive copy.

legacyReferences.role: warm human moments and reassuring service tone

legacyReferences.label: Pinterest
legacyReferences.role: soft visual discovery and personal aspiration

legacyReferences.label: Starbucks
legacyReferences.role: daily ritual and familiar personal warmth

directions.name: Executive KPI Briefing
risks: Oversimplified KPIs can conceal operational causes and uncertainty.
directions.firstViewport: Briefing-style dashboard with outcome headline, target variance, three strategic pillars, and a concise decision queue.
layoutRules: Limit the first view to decision-grade measures.
Pair each variance with context, owner, and recommended action.
directions.typography: Executive-readable headings with prominent numerals and concise analytical notes.

legacyReferences.label: IBM
legacyReferences.role: executive information hierarchy and analytical restraint

directions.name: Field Operations Mapboard
risks: Dense overlays can make geographic relationships unreadable.
directions.firstViewport: Map-dominant operations shell with regional status, live assignments, route exceptions, and a compact dispatch queue.
layoutRules: Keep spatial state primary and lists synchronized to map selection.
Make exceptions actionable without covering the map.
directions.typography: Compact field-operations UI with readable location codes and timestamps.

directions.name: Incident Response Wallboard
risks: Excessive alarm color creates fatigue and weakens severity distinctions.
directions.firstViewport: Dark operational wallboard with active incidents, affected services, response timeline, severity filters, and ownership.
layoutRules: Reserve the strongest contrast for active customer impact.
Keep time, owner, and next action visible in every incident row.
directions.typography: High-contrast operational type with tabular time and terse incident labels.

directions.name: Data Dashboard Command Center
risks: Easy to overfill with fake metrics; seed data must feel domain-specific.
directions.firstViewport: Dense but ordered shell with metric summary, chart, table, filters, and selected state.
layoutRules: Use tables and charts where the workflow needs them.
Make density scannable, not decorative.
directions.typography: Compact UI typography with strong numeric emphasis.

directions.name: Developer API Observability Lab
risks: Too many equal-weight metrics can hide the incident that needs attention.
directions.firstViewport: Operational dashboard with service health, latency trend, error groups, trace filters, and a selected incident.
layoutRules: Anchor the hierarchy on actionable anomalies and time ranges.
Use semantic color only for status and severity.
directions.typography: Dense product typography with tabular numerals and monospace identifiers.

legacyReferences.label: Sentry
legacyReferences.role: actionable error groups and incident severity

directions.name: Developer CLI Workbench
risks: Dense terminal surfaces can intimidate newcomers without progressive disclosure.
directions.firstViewport: Documentation shell with a decisive install command, workflow steps, keyboard hints, and a visible terminal result.
layoutRules: Treat commands and output as primary content, not decoration.
Keep navigation compact and make copy actions unmistakable.
directions.typography: Compact sans-serif UI paired with a highly legible monospace command layer.

legacyReferences.label: OpenCode
legacyReferences.role: open developer-tool positioning and code-native detail

legacyReferences.label: Warp
legacyReferences.role: terminal workflow clarity and command-first interaction

directions.name: Developer Dashboard Grid Data
avoidFor: Interfaces whose primary emphasis conflicts with this direction.

risks: Balanced visual treatment still requires domain-specific content and evidence.

layoutRules: Use an ordered metric and workflow grid with visible operational state.
Keep metrics, trends, and decision signals visually dominant.
Use compact spacing while preserving scan paths and target separation.
Use restrained motion for continuity, feedback, and state changes only.

Use restrained motion for continuity, feedback, and state changes only.

directions.name: Kids Exploration Path

layoutRules: Use large targets, short language, and one task per moment.
